title: cedrus libanitica var. sargentii
intro: Cedrus libanitica var. sargentii, also known as the Lebanon cedar or Sargent cedar, is a variety of cedar tree native to the mountainous regions of Lebanon and Syria. The wood of this plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to aid in the healing of wounds and reduce swelling. The essential oil extracted from Cedrus libanitica var. sargentii has been used to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and has also been found to exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant, including essential oils, extracts, and teas, have been used to promote overall health and well-being, and are often used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
